claim_text: A screenshot claims a Chrome plus Gemini browser integration bug exposed browsing context to prompt injection or leakage.
notes: Provisional paraphrase from operator brief; confirm exact exploit scope against primary vendor material.

claim_text: A screenshot claims browser AI features make every visited website able to read private assistant context.
notes: Strong universal wording is likely broader than what a specific bug report can establish.

claim_text: A screenshot claims Persona style age verification requires face or selfie capture and therefore creates a biometric risk surface.
notes: Risk surface claim may be directionally valid, but retention and sharing behavior require provider documentation.

claim_text: A screenshot claims age verification vendors keep identity images longer than users expect by default.
notes: Retention practice cannot be inferred from a screenshot without policy text or contract terms.

claim_text: A screenshot claims online age gates create a reusable surveillance rail across the wider web.
notes: May overstate from one implementation to all deployments; keep as rhetorical-risk example.

claim_text: A screenshot claims Italy moved against Perplexity specifically because of copyright violations.
notes: Regulatory action may involve multiple theories; copyright as sole ground needs primary notice.

claim_text: A screenshot claims current AI copyright lawsuits will immediately block frontier model launches.
notes: Conflates active litigation with certain immediate product shutdown.

claim_text: A screenshot claims a Seedance style video model was trained on protected works without consent.
notes: Training data provenance requires primary disclosure, litigation record, or reproducible evidence.

claim_text: A screenshot claims a Pentagon or DHS contract proves full approval for autonomous offensive AI use.
notes: Procurement engagement does not by itself prove offensive autonomy approval.

claim_text: A screenshot claims defense procurement red lines still allow analysis and support uses while restricting offensive autonomy.
notes: Directionally plausible but wording needs the actual policy or contract artifact.

claim_text: A screenshot claims local AI will eliminate junior knowledge work first.
notes: Projection claim, not a verified incident.

claim_text: A screenshot claims cloud copilots already replace entire support teams end to end.
notes: Likely overgeneralizes from isolated workflow reductions to whole-team replacement.

claim_text: A screenshot claims one AI query always consumes a bottle of water.
notes: Absolute per-query framing is usually too coarse and context-free.

claim_text: A screenshot claims frontier AI scaling is constrained by high-bandwidth memory and accelerator supply.
notes: Widely reported market condition; exact severity still depends on time and vendor.

claim_text: A screenshot claims local AI fully solves privacy, copyright, and surveillance concerns.
notes: Local execution changes exposure profile but does not fully remove licensing or misuse concerns.

claim_text: A screenshot claims cloud AI always exfiltrates user prompts for model training.
notes: Universal always-true wording needs explicit provider policy evidence and exceptions review.

claim_text: A screenshot claims AI providers sell prompt data to third parties by default.
notes: High-stakes monetization claim requires primary privacy policy or enforcement record.

claim_text: A screenshot claims consumer AI regulators in Europe have already intervened against AI services over privacy concerns.
notes: General statement is well grounded, but any service-specific framing still needs the exact notice.
